---
name: Onboarding question
about: You got stuck setting W.A.Y? up — especially around the self-definition step
labels: onboarding
---

<!--
Onboarding is the one place that needs you, so getting stuck here is expected, not a
failure. The hard part — reading your context and drafting your model — is meant to be
done for you by the extractor; your job is to review and approve. If that handoff
broke down, tell us where. See ONBOARDING.en.md for the full walkthrough.
-->

## Where you got stuck

Which onboarding step were you on? (Check the closest one.)

- [ ] Step 1 — Clone
- [ ] Step 2 — Install plugins (optional)
- [ ] Step 3 — Run the self-definition extractor (`sde-extractor`)
- [ ] Reviewing / approving the drafted `self/self-definition.md`
- [ ] Something after onboarding (first real task, full-loop, etc.)
- [ ] Not sure

## What you expected to happen

For example: "the extractor would read my CLI's memory and draft a self-definition I
could review," or "I'd see a file appear under `self/`."

## What actually happened

The error, the empty output, the confusing prompt — describe it in plain language.
If the `sde-extractor` ran but produced nothing useful, or the draft
`self/self-definition.md` looked wrong, say what felt off.

## Environment

- **CLI agent**: <!-- Claude Code, or another CLI agent -->
- **OS**: <!-- e.g. macOS, Linux, Windows -->
- **Optional plugins installed**: <!-- insane-search, deep-research, or none -->

## What you've already tried

Re-running the extractor, re-reading `ONBOARDING.en.md`, editing the draft by hand,
etc. — so nobody suggests something you already did.

> **Heads-up on privacy:** the extractor reads your local context to draft your model,
> but that draft is **yours to review before anything is committed**. When you paste
> output into this issue, redact anything personal — real names, emails, private
> numbers, the actual contents of your self-definition. A description of the problem is
> enough; we don't need your personal data to help.
